content.rendered:
A Labor Day special today, courtesy of Canadian singer-songwriter Melissa McClelland.

If her name sounds vaguely familiar and her voice sounds very familiar, that’s probably because she frequently accompanies Sarah McLachlan on tour, playing the bass guitar and providing powerful backing vocals. If you’ve seen Sarah before, you’ve likely seen Melissa.

Melissa’s solo albums are well worth seeking out, particularly her 2004 sophomore album, Stranded in Suburbia. Right at the heart of an album of beautiful original material is a lone cover: Bruce Springsteen’s “Factory.”

Arranged as a crunchy “Death to My Hometown”-esque work stomp, Melissa pulls off a tough feat–capturing the song’s original tone while hardening it into a true rocker.

Enjoy the day off if you have it, and send thanks to those who don’t. Our essential workers are what today’s all about.

© September 7, 2020

excerpt.rendered: A Labor Day special, courtesy of Canadian singer-songwriter Melissa McClellan’s rocking arrangement of “Factory.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Melissa Etheridge: Thunder Road
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Straight from her daily stay-at-home Facebook Live concerts, here's Melissa Etheridge's gorgeous rendition of "Thunder Road."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Any Other Way
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Even Bruce Springsteen's musical encyclopedia can miss an entry or two, as evidenced by his adaptation of this William Bell song transformed by Jackie Shane.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Lucinda Williams: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Lucinda Williams covers Bruce in concert often, but she's only covered him in the studio once. Take a listen to her version of "Factory" from her 2016 album, The Ghost of Highway 20.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, The Deep Dark Woods: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Canadian band Deep Dark Woods turns in a faithful cover of Bruce's minimalist musing on the meaning of work.

jetpack-related-posts.title: The Descendants: Land of Hope and Dreams
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: South African folk combo The Descendants turn in an inspirational cover of one of Bruce's very best songs.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Fabio Melis: Factory
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Fabio Melis turns in a strong Cover Me contest contender with a rock arrangement of "Factory" that's more E Street than the E Street Band's version.
